name: specforge-ai
description: Minimal VS Code extension for exploring SpecForge.AI user stories.

specForge.execution.modelProfiles.description: Named model profiles. Each profile declares its own provider, endpoint, credentials, and concrete model so the workflow can route phases explicitly.

name.description: Stable profile name used by phase assignments.

provider.description: Provider kind used by this profile. `codex`, `copilot`, and `claude` currently use the same OpenAI-compatible transport bridge while preserving provider identity for routing and audit.

baseUrl.description: Base URL for this profile's endpoint.

apiKey.description: API key for this profile's endpoint. Leave empty only for trusted local endpoints such as Ollama.

model.description: Concrete model identifier exposed by this profile's endpoint.

repositoryAccess.description: Repository access granted to this profile. Implementation requires read-write. Review requires at least read.

specForge.execution.phaseModels.description: Assign named model profiles to workflow roles. Unassigned implementation or review phases fall back to the default profile.

defaultProfile.description: Profile used by capture, clarification, refinement, technical design, release approval, and PR preparation.

implementationProfile.description: Optional profile override for implementation. This is the place for the strongest model.

reviewProfile.description: Optional profile override for review. Falls back to the default profile when unset.

specForge.execution.clarificationToleran.description: How demanding clarification should be before the workflow advances to refinement. This also adjusts the temperature sent for the clarification phase.

specForge.execution.reviewTolerance.description: How demanding the review phase should be when assessing findings and pass/fail readiness. This also adjusts the temperature sent for the review phase.

specForge.ui.enableWatcher.description: Refresh the explorer and workflow view automatically when .specs files change.

specForge.ui.notifyOnAttention.description: Show notifications when a user story becomes waiting-user, blocked, or completed.

specForge.features.enableContextSuggesti.description: Suggest repository context files during clarification using local heuristics and repo neighborhood rules.

specForge.features.requireApprovalBranch.description: Require explicit acceptance of the proposed base branch in the workflow detail before approving refinement.

specForge.features.autoPlayEnabled.description: Automatically resume workflow playback after manual actions such as approving refinement when the next phase can continue.

specForge.features.destructiveRewindEnab.description: When enabled, rewind and regress delete later derived artifacts and branch metadata. When disabled, both only move the workflow state back.
